content.problemStatement:
Gru has a string SSS of length NNN, consisting of only characters aaa and bbb for banana and PPP points to spend.

Now Gru wants to replace and/or re-arrange characters of this given string to get the lexicographically smallest string possible. For this, he can perform the following two operations any number of times.



Swap any two characters in the string. This operation costs 111 pointpointpoint. (any two, need not be adjacent)




Replace a character in the string with any other lower case english letter. This operation costs 222 pointspointspoints.



Help Gru in obtaining the lexicographically smallest string possible, by using at most PPP points.

###Input:


First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains two lines of input, first-line containing two integers NNN , PPP.
The second line contains a string SSS consisting of NNN characters.
###Output:
For each testcase, output in a single containing the lexicographically smallest string obtained.

###Constraints


1≤T≤101 \leq T \leq 101≤T≤10
1≤N≤1051 \leq N \leq 10^51≤N≤105
0≤P≤2N0 \leq P \leq 2N0≤P≤2N
SSS only consists of ′a′'a'′a′ and ′b′'b'′b′
###Sample Input:
1
3 3
bba

###Sample Output:
aab

###Explanation:
We swap S[0]S[0]S[0] and S[2]S[2]S[2], to get abbabbabb. With the 2 remaining points, we replace S[1]S[1]S[1] to obtain aabaabaab which is the lexicographically smallest string possible for this case.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T. Each test case contains two lines of input: first line contains two integers N and P, and the second line contains a string S consisting of N characters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output in a single line the lexicographically smallest string obtained.

sampleTestCases.explanation: We swap S[0] and S[2] to get abbabbabb. With the 2 remaining points, we replace S[1] to obtain aabaabaab which is the lexicographically smallest string possible for this case.
We replace S[3] and S[4] to obtain aabaa which is the lexicographically smallest string possible for this case.
We replace S[6] and S[7] to obtain aababababa which is the lexicographically smallest string possible for this case.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by first counting the number of 'a's and 'b's in the string. Then, we can replace 'b's with 'a's until we run out of points or we have no more 'b's to replace. Finally, we can sort the remaining characters in lexicographical order.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) due to sorting
